standing: FRESH build — born and cast (ticket cairn-command) the same session from an /intent bundling a command-runner, an intentions updater, and a repo-scanner; the prefix question resolved by measuring PATH conflicts and placing the ergonomic shorthand on the instance side (full `cairn` in git, personal alias in dotfiles).

A set -e exit-code bug found by the proof at build (the usage-lister's trailing && echo short-circuited to exit 1 before reaching exit 127/2, carrying the wrong loud-fail code) was fixed by making the lister always return 0, then re-proven.

BUILT and PROVEN — green bare and sealed under the tester with VALIDATION persisted beside-code: `cairn compile` runs the compiler end to end, an unknown name exits 127 with a legible message, no args exits 2 with the command list, and argv reaches the subcommand verbatim with the exit code propagated.
